catalog_info.title: Stormwater Heatmap Data Layers
catalog_info.description: Full-extent raster data for Puget Sound hydrology and pollutants, including land cover, runoff, and pollution concentrations.

layers.title: Age of Imperviousness
layers.description: Provides multi-temporal information on the presence of built-up surfaces derived from global Landsat satellite data collected from 1975 to 2014. Used to estimate current stormwater treatment levels.

layers.title: Flow Duration Index
layers.description: A dimensionless index indicating the level of flow control needed to match forest hydrology. It represents the magnitude of change to the flow-duration curve between flow thresholds.

layers.title: HSPF Land Cover Type
layers.description: Land cover classified by categories used in the Western Washington Hydrology Model (WWHM). Derived from 1-meter resolution data.

layers.title: Hydrologic Response Units
layers.description: Landscape units based on common soils, land cover, and slope characteristics. Used for computationally efficient pre-computing of hydrologic response. 101 = Type C soils, forested, moderate slope.

layers.title: Total Copper Concentration
layers.description: Predicted pollutant concentrations derived from linear mixed-effects models using spatial covariates.

layers.title: Total Phosphorus Concentration
layers.description: Predicted pollutant concentrations derived from linear mixed-effects models using spatial covariates.

layers.title: Traffic ADT
layers.description: High-resolution raster dataset of Annual Daily Traffic (ADT) across road surface areas.
layers.methodology: Uses a distance decay function for local roads where $r = f(a) = a 	imes (11.03955 - 0.007743235a + 0.000001332168a^2)$.
